water.name: Upper Truckee River and tributaries upstream from confluence with Showers Creek

authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(161) — Upper Truckee River and tributaries upstream from confluence with Showers Creek
sources.quotedText: (161) Upper Truckee River and tributaries upstream from confluence with Showers Creek (Alpine and El Dorado Cos.). Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 236928, "Upper Truckee River", Stream, Alpine/El Dorado Cos., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":236928,"gaz_name":"Upper Truckee River","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Alpine"}. Corrects prior file coordinates (-120.0160, 38.7400), which did not match any of GNIS's recorded multipoint vertices for the river (nearest was ~1.4 km away) and appear to have been guessed. The corrected value (-119.989084, 38.718246) is GNIS's southernmost/source vertex for the river, matching Wikipedia's cited source coordinate (38.71833, -119.98917) near Red Lake Peak/Meiss Meadows — the headwater reach this water's name and 'Meiss Meadows reach' alias refer to, upstream of the Showers Creek confluence.

regulations.humanSummary: Open Saturday preceding Memorial Day through September 30
regulations.verbatimText: Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30.

parameters.note: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Artificial lures with barbless hooks only
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: 0 trout — zero bag limit; catch-and-release only, protecting the reintroduced Lahontan cutthroat trout population above the natural barrier near the Showers Creek confluence.

regulations.humanSummary: Zero trout bag limit — catch and release only

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
